releases.title: SFTP over SSH, auto-updates, and cleaner licensing

highlights.title: SFTP over SSH
highlights.body: Alongside FTP and FTPS, Pier now speaks true SSH-based SFTP. Pick "SFTP (SSH)" from the protocol dropdown when adding a connection — port 22 fills in automatically.

highlights.title: Auto-update
highlights.body: Pier now checks for new versions in the background and offers to install them with one click. Never re-download manually again. Weekly checks by default; manual "Check for Updates…" lives under the Pier menu.

highlights.title: License paste strips wrapped newlines
highlights.body: Emails that word-wrap your key across two lines no longer cause activation errors. Just paste and activate.

highlights.title: Menu says "Pier"
highlights.body: About / Quit / Hide menu items now read "Pier" (they read "FTP Manager" in 1.0). Cosmetic, but overdue.

highlights.title: Better error classification
highlights.body: Authentication failures over SSH are correctly identified as auth errors (not transient network issues), so the circuit breaker doesn't open on wrong-password attempts.

highlights.title: Initial release
highlights.body: Native macOS app with embedded Node backend. Signed with Developer ID, notarized by Apple, universal (Apple Silicon + Intel).

highlights.title: FTP / FTPS connections
highlights.body: Plain FTP and FTPS (explicit TLS), with encrypted credential storage and a circuit breaker that protects you from fail2ban bans on shared hosts.

highlights.title: AI-driven chat
highlights.body: Powered by Claude Opus 4.7 (Sonnet 4.6 optional). Describe what you want done, Pier reads the files it needs and proposes every change as a reviewable diff.

highlights.title: Safe edits
highlights.body: Every file write is a proposal you approve. Auto-backup before upload, 10-second undo toast, and up to 90-day per-file version history.

highlights.title: Site health audits
highlights.body: SEO, security, performance, accessibility, broken links, meta descriptions, viewport, analytics. Each returns issues plus auto-fix proposals where safe.

highlights.title: MySQL database bridge
highlights.body: Discover databases from wp-config, configuration.php, .env. Read-only by default; row-level updates and deletes behind an explicit confirmation.

highlights.title: Free 30-page user guide
highlights.body: Full walkthrough for non-coders, in English and Dutch. Available from the download page.
